chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يَجُوزُ لِلْمُحْرِمَةِ أَنْ تَلْبَسَهُ مِنَ الثِّيَابِ وَ الْحُلِيِّ وَ مَا يُكْرَهُ لَهَا مِنْ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 85 – What is allowed for the female in Ihraam to wear from the clothes, and the ornaments, and what is disliked for her from that

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/293)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/293)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: مَرَّ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) بِامْرَأَةٍ مُحْرِمَةٍ قَدِ اسْتَتَرَتْ بِمِرْوَحَةٍ فَأَمَاطَ الْمِرْوَحَةَ بِنَفْسِهِ عَنْ وَجْهِهَا .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassanasws having said: ‘Abu Ja’far passed by a woman in Ihraam who had veiled with a vent, so heasws removed the vent himselfasws from her face’.
en.sarwar: 9. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, once when passing by a woman, who in the state of Ihram had covered her face with an air vent, himself removed it from her face.’”
